industry.headline: Karnataka Retains ±5% Deviation Settlement Band for Solar Projects
industry.summary: Karnataka Electricity Regulatory Commission (KERC) has retained the deviation settlement mechanism (DSM) tolerance band for solar projects at ±5%, rejecting requests to maintain ±10% for two years. In April 2026, the Karnataka High Court stayed the enforcement of CERC’s revised DSM norms, which include the narrower ±5% band, currently allowing developers to comply with earlier norms.

learn.title: Demand Response Explained
learn.intro: Demand response helps balance power grids by adjusting large consumer usage. It's like turning down the thermostat when electricity is scarce.
learn.c1_text: When demand drops, grid operators can reduce loads quickly, preventing blackouts and saving money.
learn.c2_text: A Power Purchase Agreement lets renewable projects secure loans by showing long-term contracts.
learn.c3_text: Capacity factor shows how often a plant operates at full power, often lower than rated due to weather or demand.
learn.stat1: India has 90 GW solar installed in 2024
learn.stat2: Average capacity factor for Indian solar is around 25-30%
learn.stat3: Utility-scale wind capacity in India reached 42 GW by 2023

learn.title: Power Purchase Agreement Overview
learn.intro: RE projects need financing through agreements that guarantee buyers can purchase power.
learn.c1_text: Projects rely on PPAs to attract investors and lock in revenue streams.
learn.c2_text: PPAs can be fixed price or variable, depending on market conditions and project needs.

learn.title: Capacity Factor Meaning
learn.intro: Understanding capacity factor helps show how efficiently renewable plants generate power.
learn.c1_text: A higher capacity factor means more consistent power output per installed capacity.
learn.c2_text: India's solar plants typically operate at 25-30% capacity factor due to seasonal variations.
learn.stat3: India's solar capacity grew 60% from 2018 to 2022
learn.stat4: Capacity factor for utility solar averages 20% in southern states
learn.stat5: Wind capacity factor in India often exceeds 40% in windy regions
learn.readLabel: Read at US Energy Info Admin

learn.title: EV Motor Operation Basics
learn.intro: Electric vehicles use motors and regenerative braking to move efficiently.
learn.c1_text: Motors convert electrical energy into motion, just like in traditional engines.
learn.c2_text: When braking, EVs convert kinetic energy back to stored electricity, saving power.

learn.title: Battery Management Systems
learn.intro: BMS protects and optimizes batteries for safety and longevity in EVs.
learn.c1_text: BMS monitors temperature, voltage, and state of charge to extend battery life.
learn.c2_text: Without BMS, batteries would degrade faster, increasing costs and reducing usable energy.
learn.stat4: BMS reduces battery degradation by up to 30%
learn.stat5: India's electric vehicle market reached 1.5 million units in 2023
learn.stat6: Global EV batteries use BMS to manage charge cycles
learn.readLabel: Read at US Dept of Energy

learn.title: LCOE Comparison Guide
learn.intro: LCOE helps compare the true cost of different energy sources for investment decisions.
learn.c1_text: Levelized Cost of Energy shows average lifetime costs per kWh for comparison.
learn.c2_text: IRENA reports LCOE for solar dropped 89% from 2010 to 2023.
learn.stat3: The LCOE of utility solar is now below $30 per MWh
learn.stat4: Onshore wind is often cheaper than coal in many markets
learn.stat5: LCOE for onshore wind in India is around $0.02-$0.05 per kWh
